title: Expose authenticated wishlist Store API
verify: GET/POST/DELETE routes require standard Medusa customer actor context; production storefront uses session cookie only.
Customer ID is derived only from auth context and never accepted from request input.
List/add use the same exact item/projection shape; all hidden product cases use indistinguishable 404 wishlist_product_not_found while out-of-stock remains visible.
Two-customer tests prove one actor cannot list or remove the other's favorite; synthetic bearer is limited to the existing local harness and adds no production mechanism.
evidence_required: Guest and production-style session API integration output, plus existing local harness synthetic-bearer coverage where needed.
Exact item shape, unified hidden 404, out-of-stock, and idempotency assertions.
HUMAN_CHECKPOINT and rollback/recovery evidence required by T3 policy.
constraints: Use standard Medusa customer middleware and req.auth_context.actor_id; production storefront credential remains session cookie.
Do not add production bearer configuration, storage, or client behavior; only the existing local harness may supply a synthetic bearer.
Pass publishable-key sales channel to product visibility validation.
Return only the exact projection, stable sanitized errors, and actor-visible data.
invariants: No request field can select customer owner.
Product ID alone cannot reveal or remove another customer's record.
Missing, unpublished, channel-invisible, and inactive-category add failures are the same 404.
Guest requests create no wishlist state.
purpose: Expose wishlist behavior through an authenticated, ownership-safe Store boundary.
success_outcome: Authenticated customers can list/add/remove only their own favorites, while guest and cross-customer attempts fail without leakage.
anti_goals: Do not implement authentication providers/session creation or a new production bearer path.
Do not accept customer ID or record owner from client.

stop_conditions: Authenticated customer actor is unavailable through standard Medusa middleware.
Ownership cannot be enforced without client-supplied customer ID.
Exact item projection or unified hidden 404 cannot be preserved.
Implementation would add a production bearer mechanism or expose cross-customer/sensitive data.
